Document:
# I.P.C.
An IPC (short for Integrated Positronic Chassis) is a type of sentient robot and is considered an [color=yellow]independent individual[/color], meaning [color=#ec3a3a]they are not guided by any laws of robotics[/color]. IPCs cannot be hacked by Emags because they do not have to follow any predefined directives in their system. [color=#ec3a3a]IPCs are silicon-based beings[/color], [color=#ec3a3a]so they are usually healed by Science or Engineering instead of Medical[/color].
Like borgs, IPCs have a positronic brain as their processing source. However, unlike them, IPCs can't be assembled. "It's cheaper to create a shell that obeys you than one that doesn't! *wink*"
## Benefits - [color=#1e90ff]20% resistance[/color] to [color=#ec3a3a]Brute[/color] damage. - [color=#1e90ff]60% resistance[/color] to [color=#ec3a3a]Radiation[/color] damage. - [color=#1e90ff]Able to pull[/color] lighter species and objects [color=#1e90ff]more effectively[/color]. - [color=#1e90ff]Do not need to breathe[/color] and [color=#1e90ff]do not take Pressure damage[/color].
## Drawbacks - [color=#ec3a3a]70% more vulnerable[/color] to [color=#ec3a3a]Heat[/color] damage. - [color=#ec3a3a]100% more vulnerable[/color] to [color=#ec3a3a]Shock[/color] damage. - [color=#ec3a3a]Cannot be healed through Medical means[/color], must be repaired using welders and wire. - [color=yellow]Heavy alloy construction[/color] makes them [color=#ec3a3a]harder to pull[/color]. - [bold][color=#ec3a3a]Die at 100 total damage[/color][/bold] and have no critical state. - [color=#ec3a3a]Vulnerable to electric disruption[/color] such as EMPs
## Recharging an IPC
IPCs can be recharged in three different ways:
[bold]APC Terminal:[/bold] IPCs can use APC terminals to recharge. Press [color=yellow]Alt + left click[/color] on a terminal as many times as needed to fully recharge.
[bold]Borg Rechargers:[/bold] IPCs can use borg rechargers to recharge. Always prioritize the ones outside of the Sci area to avoid headaches.
[bold]Power Cell:[/bold] IPCs have an internal power cell that serves as their battery. They can simply swap it out by opening the hatch and manually replacing it.
## Integrated Radio
IPCs do [bold]not[/bold] use external radios because they already have one built in. They only need to get an encryption key from a radio. By clicking on an IPC with a [color=yellow]wire cutter[/color], you can remove their keys. You can find new keys around the station or remove one from a sector radio using a [color=yellow]screwdriver[/color].
## Repairing
Welders can be used to repair [color=yellow]Brute[/color] damage.
Cables can be used to repair [color=yellow]Burns[/color].
Glass Sheets can be used to repair [color=yellow]Blindness[/color].
In the event an IPC dies, after being fully repaired, it should be restarted using the [color=yellow][bold]Restart[/bold][/color] button (located by right-clicking).
[bold][color=red]NEVER ELECTROCUTE AN IPC with a defibrillator or in any other way while it is dead, as this will cause the battery to discharge energy rays outward![/color][/bold]
